---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "from_cells2stats_to_h5mu"
namespace: "Convert"
description: "Convert spatial data resulting from Aviti Teton sequencers that have been processed by the Element Biosciences cells2stats workflow to H5MU format."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `from_cells2stats_to_h5mu`  
Namespace: `convert`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu)

:::

This component processes cells2stats count matrices to create a standardized H5MU file for downstream analysis.

The component reads:
- Parquet file containing the count matrix and metadata
- Panel.json with target and batch information

And outputs an H5MU file with:
- Count data as the main .X matrix
- Spatial coordinates in obsm
- Cell Paint intensities in obsm (optional)
- Nuclear count data as a layer (optional)
- CellProfiler morphology metrics in obsm (optional)
- Unassigned targets in obsm (optional)


## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name      |Description                                                                                                                                                                             |Attributes                                          |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input` |Path to the cells2stats output bundle.  Expected folder structure (showing required files only): ├── Cytoprofiling/ │   └── Instrument/ │       └── RawCellStats.parquet └── Panel.json |`file`, required, example: `"path/to/aviti_output"` |


### Outputs

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                   |Attributes                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`             |Output H5MU file path.                                                                                        |`file`, required, example: `"output.h5mu"` |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata objects. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                |


### Options

|Name                        |Description                                                                                                                                                                                                              |Attributes                                |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--modality`                |The modality to which the processed data will be written to in the H5MU file.                                                                                                                                            |`string`, default: `"rna"`                |
|`--obsm_coordinates`        |Key name to store the spatial coordinates (in pixels) in obsm. If present, spatial coordinates in micrometers will be stored under {obsm_coordinates}_um. The column names will be stored in uns.                        |`string`, default: `"spatial"`            |
|`--layer_nuclear_counts`    |Name for nuclear counts layer. If specified, nuclear count data  will be stored as a separate layer in the AnnData object.                                                                                               |`string`, example: `"nuclear_counts"`     |
|`--obsm_cell_paint`         |Key name for storing Cell Paint target intensities in obsm.  If provided, Cell Paint target intensity data will be stored as a separate matrix in the obsm field. The column names will be stored in uns.                |`string`, example: `"cell_paint"`         |
|`--obsm_cell_paint_nuclear` |Key name for storing Nuclear Cell Paint target intensities in obsm. If provided, Nuclear Cell Paint target intensity data will be stored as a separate matrix in the obsm field. The column names will be stored in uns. |`string`, example: `"cell_paint_nuclear"` |
|`--obsm_cell_profiler`      |Key name for storing CellProfiler morphology metrics in obsm. If provided, CellProfiler morphology metrics will be stored as a separate matrix in the obsm field. The column names will be stored in uns.                |`string`, example: `"cell_profiler"`      |
|`--obsm_unassigned_targets` |Key name for storing any unassigned target data in obsm. If provided, unassigned target data will be stored as a separate matrix in the obsm field. The column names will be stored in uns.                              |`string`, example: `"cell_profiler"`      |

## Authors


  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (maintainer)
